name: Harith ibn Nabhan
title: Relic of a Companion
role: Attendant of the Master of Martyrs
shortDescription: The son of Nabhan (servant of Hamza Sayyid al-Shuhada) who accompanied Imam Hussain (AS) from Medina to Karbala and was martyred on Ashura.
fullStory:
Harith ibn Nabhan was the son of Nabhan, the brave servant of Hamza Sayyid al-Shuhada (the Prophet's uncle). Nabhan himself was a warrior who made many sacrifices in the Battle of Uhud. Harith inherited this nobility and bravery from his father and was always an attendant and lover of the Prophet's (PBUH) family.

When Imam Hussain (AS) decided to leave Medina for Mecca and then Karbala, Harith ibn Nabhan immediately joined him. Throughout the journey, he was a devoted servant and attendant of the Hussaini caravan.

On the day of Ashura, with the start of Yazid's army's all-out attack, Harith, like the other dedicated companions of the Imam, drew his sword. Having been raised in the household of the prophecy, he entered the battlefield with absolute valor.

After a manifest battle and taking down a number of Kufans, he too attained the grace of martyrdom in that very first attack and joined his brave father in Paradise.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى الحارِثِ بْنِ نَبْهانَ مَوْلى حَمْزَةَ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Harith ibn Nabhan, the servant of Hamza.
